2026-04-15T22:53:36.4305390Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-15T22:53:36.4305856Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-15T22:53:36.4306261Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-15T22:53:36.4306573Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-15T22:53:36.4306806Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-15T22:56:41.7727818Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-15T22:54:43.374124873Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58190,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations README missing InvocationContext properties and manual DI setup\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit 5dd1443c4 (PR #58178) by `@azure-sdk`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPost-release version increment of `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` from `1.0.0-beta.1` → `1.0.0-beta.2` following the initial package release on 2026-04-14 as part of the major three-package architectural rewrite (`Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` beta.21).\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `InvocationContext.InvocationId` is only mentioned in passing in the \\u0026#34;Handler lifetime\\u0026#34; section and is not described in the **InvocationContext** key concepts subsection — important since `GetAsync` and `CancelAsync` both receive an `invocationId` parameter that correlates with this value.\\n- `InvocationContext.QueryParameters` (`IReadOnlyDictionary`) is not mentioned anywhere in the README.\\n- `InvocationContext.Isolation` (`IsolationContext`) is not mentioned anywhere in the README — leaving multi-tenant scenarios (user/chat isolation keys) completely undocumented.\\n- No manual ASP.NET Core DI setup example — the Responses README shows both `ResponsesServer.Run()` and a manual `WebApplication.CreateBuilder()` approach (`AddResponsesServer()` + `MapResponsesServer()`), but the Invocations README shows only `InvocationsServer.Run()`. The equivalent manual setup (`AddInvocationsServer()` + `MapInvocationsServer()`) is missing.\\n- `InvocationsServerOptions` is part of the public API but is never mentioned or described in the README.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/README.md` — Edit\\n\\n#### 1a. Expand the `InvocationContext` key concept subsection\\n\\nReplace the existing `### InvocationContext` subsection (currently two sentences):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### InvocationContext\\n\\nProvides request metadata to the handler, including the resolved session ID and forwarded client headers.\\n```\\n\\nwith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### InvocationContext\\n\\nProvides request metadata to the handler. All properties are read-only and resolved before `HandleAsync` is called.\\n\\n| Property | Type | Description |\\n|----------|------|-------------|\\n| `InvocationId` | `string` | Unique identifier for this invocation. Passed as the first argument to `GetAsync` and `CancelAsync`. Use as a key when storing per-invocation state. |\\n| `SessionId` | `string` | Resolved multi-turn session identifier (extracted from query parameters or `INVOCATIONS_SESSION_ID` environment variable). |\\n| `ClientHeaders` | `IReadOnlyDictionary` | Forwarded `x-client-*` headers from the original request — useful for propagating tracing context and client metadata. |\\n| `QueryParameters` | `IReadOnlyDictionary` | All query parameters from the incoming request. |\\n| `Isolation` | `IsolationContext` | Isolation context extracted from `x-agent-user-isolation-key` and `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` headers. Useful for multi-tenant scenarios where per-user or per-chat data must be isolated. `IsolationContext.Empty` indicates no isolation headers were present. |\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Add a manual DI setup example in `### Configure the server`\\n\\nAfter the existing Tier 1 snippet block and `EchoHandler` code block (after the closing paragraph about `AgentHostBuilder`), add a new subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\nFor full control over the ASP.NET Core host, register services manually in your `Program.cs`:\\n\\n```\\nvar builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder();\\n\\nbuilder.Services.AddInvocationsServer();\\nbuilder.Services.AddScoped();\\n\\nvar app = builder.Build();\\napp.MapInvocationsServer();\\napp.Run();\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### 1c. Document `InvocationsServerOptions` in the `### Customizing the host` subsection\\n\\nAppend after the existing tier sample links:\\n\\n```markdown\\nTo configure `InvocationsServerOptions` (for future options), pass a configure delegate:\\n\\n```\\nInvocationsServer.Run(configure: builder =\\u0026gt;\\n{\\n    builder.AddInvocations(options =\\u0026gt;\\n    {\\n        // InvocationsServerOptions configuration here\\n    });\\n});\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n#### Snippet: `Invocations_ReadMe_ManualSetup`\\n\\n**Test file:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/tests/Snippets/ReadMeSnippets.cs`\\n\\nAdd a new test method to the existing `ReadMeSnippets.cs` file:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\npublic async Task ManualSetupSnippet()\\n{\\n    #region Snippet:Invocations_ReadMe_ManualSetup\\n    var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder();\\n\\n    builder.Services.AddInvocationsServer();\\n    builder.Services.AddScoped();\\n\\n    var app = builder.Build();\\n    app.MapInvocationsServer();\\n    app.Run();\\n    #endregion\\n\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n    // Test-only: just verify compilation; 2026-04-15T22:56:41.7786836Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-15T22:54:43.588519476Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58190,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations README missing InvocationContext properties and manual DI setup\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit 5dd1443c4 (PR #58178) by `@azure-sdk`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPost-release version increment of `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` from `1.0.0-beta.1` → `1.0.0-beta.2` following the initial package release on 2026-04-14 as part of the major three-package architectural rewrite (`Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` beta.21).\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `InvocationContext.InvocationId` is only mentioned in passing in the \\u0026#34;Handler lifetime\\u0026#34; section and is not described in the **InvocationContext** key concepts subsection — important since `GetAsync` and `CancelAsync` both receive an `invocationId` parameter that correlates with this value.\\n- `InvocationContext.QueryParameters` (`IReadOnlyDictionary`) is not mentioned anywhere in the README.\\n- `InvocationContext.Isolation` (`IsolationContext`) is not mentioned anywhere in the README — leaving multi-tenant scenarios (user/chat isolation keys) completely undocumented.\\n- No manual ASP.NET Core DI setup example — the Responses README shows both `ResponsesServer.Run()` and a manual `WebApplication.CreateBuilder()` approach (`AddResponsesServer()` + `MapResponsesServer()`), but the Invocations README shows only `InvocationsServer.Run()`. The equivalent manual setup (`AddInvocationsServer()` + `MapInvocationsServer()`) is missing.\\n- `InvocationsServerOptions` is part of the public API but is never mentioned or described in the README.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/README.md` — Edit\\n\\n#### 1a. Expand the `InvocationContext` key concept subsection\\n\\nReplace the existing `### InvocationContext` subsection (currently two sentences):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### InvocationContext\\n\\nProvides request metadata to the handler, including the resolved session ID and forwarded client headers.\\n```\\n\\nwith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### InvocationContext\\n\\nProvides request metadata to the handler. All properties are read-only and resolved before `HandleAsync` is called.\\n\\n| Property | Type | Description |\\n|----------|------|-------------|\\n| `InvocationId` | `string` | Unique identifier for this invocation. Passed as the first argument to `GetAsync` and `CancelAsync`. Use as a key when storing per-invocation state. |\\n| `SessionId` | `string` | Resolved multi-turn session identifier (extracted from query parameters or `INVOCATIONS_SESSION_ID` environment variable). |\\n| `ClientHeaders` | `IReadOnlyDictionary` | Forwarded `x-client-*` headers from the original request — useful for propagating tracing context and client metadata. |\\n| `QueryParameters` | `IReadOnlyDictionary` | All query parameters from the incoming request. |\\n| `Isolation` | `IsolationContext` | Isolation context extracted from `x-agent-user-isolation-key` and `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` headers. Useful for multi-tenant scenarios where per-user or per-chat data must be isolated. `IsolationContext.Empty` indicates no isolation headers were present. |\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Add a manual DI setup example in `### Configure the server`\\n\\nAfter the existing Tier 1 snippet block and `EchoHandler` code block (after the closing paragraph about `AgentHostBuilder`), add a new subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\nFor full control over the ASP.NET Core host, register services manually in your `Program.cs`:\\n\\n```\\nvar builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder();\\n\\nbuilder.Services.AddInvocationsServer();\\nbuilder.Services.AddScoped();\\n\\nvar app = builder.Build();\\napp.MapInvocationsServer();\\napp.Run();\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### 1c. Document `InvocationsServerOptions` in the `### Customizing the host` subsection\\n\\nAppend after the existing tier sample links:\\n\\n```markdown\\nTo configure `InvocationsServerOptions` (for future options), pass a configure delegate:\\n\\n```\\nInvocationsServer.Run(configure: builder =\\u0026gt;\\n{\\n    builder.AddInvocations(options =\\u0026gt;\\n    {\\n        // InvocationsServerOptions configuration here\\n    });\\n});\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n#### Snippet: `Invocations_ReadMe_ManualSetup`\\n\\n**Test file:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/tests/Snippets/ReadMeSnippets.cs`\\n\\nAdd a new test method to the existing `ReadMeSnippets.cs` file:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\npublic async Task ManualSetupSnippet()\\n{\\n    #region Snippet:Invocations_ReadMe_ManualSetup\\n    var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder();\\n\\n    builder.Services.AddInvocationsServer();\\n    builder.Services.AddScoped();\\n\\n    var app = builder.Build();\\n    app.MapInvocationsServer();\\n    app.Run();\\n    #endregion\\n\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n    // Test-only: just verify compilation;
